name: Anguilla
overview:
Anguilla is a small, flat coral island in the northeastern Caribbean and one of the most beautiful destinations in the entire region. Known for having some of the finest white sand beaches in the world, crystal-clear turquoise waters and an unhurried atmosphere, it attracts discerning travelers looking for something beyond the ordinary.

Despite its small size — just 16 miles long and 3 miles wide — Anguilla punches well above its weight with world-class cuisine and extraordinary beaches. The island has no traffic lights, no fast food chains and no high-rise hotels — and that is very much by design.

topExperiences.name: Shoal Bay East
topExperiences.desc: Consistently ranked among the world's best beaches — two miles of powdery white sand and calm turquoise water.

topExperiences.name: Lobster on the Beach
topExperiences.desc: Fresh Caribbean lobster grilled at beachfront restaurants — Anguilla's culinary scene is exceptional for its size.

topExperiences.name: Sandy Island Day Trip
topExperiences.desc: A tiny uninhabited sand bar surrounded by impossibly clear turquoise water — accessible by short boat ride from Sandy Ground.

topExperiences.name: Prickly Pear Cays
topExperiences.desc: Two uninhabited cays with excellent reef snorkeling and crystal-clear water — one of the best snorkeling experiences in the Eastern Caribbean.

topExperiences.name: Sunset at Meads Bay
topExperiences.desc: Long, uncrowded beach with luxury resorts and one of the most beautiful sunsets in the Caribbean.

topExperiences.name: Traditional Boat Racing
topExperiences.desc: Anguilla's national sport — wooden sailboat races held throughout the year that offer a window into authentic island life.

beaches.name: Shoal Bay East
beaches.desc: The island's most famous beach — 2 miles of white sand and calm turquoise water.

beaches.name: Meads Bay
beaches.desc: Long stretch of white sand with Anguilla's finest resorts and restaurants.

beaches.name: Rendezvous Bay
beaches.desc: Calm, protected bay with stunning views of Saint Martin.

beaches.name: Barnes Bay
beaches.desc: Quiet and beautiful bay on the northwest coast — minimal development.

food.name: Fresh Lobster
food.desc: Spiny Caribbean lobster grilled at beachfront restaurants — Smokey's at the Cove is the classic spot.

food.name: Blanchards Restaurant
food.desc: Iconic fine dining on Meads Bay — one of the Caribbean's most celebrated restaurants. Reservation essential.

food.name: Johnny Cakes
food.desc: Fried cornmeal bread served at local snackettes — the Anguillan breakfast staple.

food.name: Pea Soup & Dumplings
food.desc: Anguilla's traditional Sunday dish — pigeon pea soup with thick dumplings, slow-cooked and deeply satisfying.

culture: Anguilla's culture is shaped by its African heritage, British colonial history and fierce island identity. The island is known for the 1967 Revolution — when Anguillans rejected incorporation into the St. Kitts-Nevis-Anguilla federation and secured their status as a British territory. Anguilla Day on May 30 commemorates this with boat races and celebration. Traditional wooden boat racing is the national sport, held year-round with passionate crowds.
bestTime: December through April is the dry season — perfect weather with minimal rain. May and November offer good weather with fewer crowds. Hurricane season runs June through October.

practical.currency: Eastern Caribbean Dollar (XCD) · USD widely accepted
practical.howToGet: No direct flights from most US cities. Connect through Sint Maarten (SXM — 25-min ferry), San Juan (SJU) or Antigua (ANU).
